relations.note: Mandatory bolding rules define the specific exceptions to the general hierarchy restrictions.

quotes: Bold is for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration.
Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.
Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler

rules.intent: Maintain typographic hierarchy and readability by restricting the overuse of bolding.
rules.rule_text: Bold is for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure. Never bold entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, prepositional filler, or more than ~15% of running body text in any section.

quotes: Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).

rules.intent: Ensure consistent brand prominence and numerical focus across email sections.
rules.rule_text: Always bold: callout text per the callout specs, statistical callout numbers, and the brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (limited to the first instance per section only). Never bold the generic name.

quotes: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

rules.intent: Avoid visual noise by restricting the stacking of multiple emphasis styles.
rules.rule_text: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

quotes: No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

rules.intent: Maintain web/email standard conventions where underlining implies a link, and prevent confusing italicization in patient-facing layouts.
rules.rule_text: Do not underline any text except real hyperlinks (body.link.text_decoration = underline). Do not use italics for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics exclusively for study names, notes ('Note:'), and citations.
